title: Biofilm growth with starch treatment
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"b7m66","text":"This is the main protocol to grow a calcifying oral biofilm model with starch treatments. It uses a 24 deepwell plate with the accompanying lid as substratum (polypropylene). The protocol takes 25 days to run, with daily tasks.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"42bk6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bh8ic","text":"Modified from protocols by Sissons et al. (1991) and Extercate et al. 2010.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"dcc92","text":"The preferred substratum for inoculation is glass or hydroxyapatite. Plastic substrata can be used but are less effective, so surface treatment of the plastic is recommeded (e.g. heated HCl or acetone).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"91o4s","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"93skr","text":"Substrata should be autoclaved prior to use, both before and during the experiment.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"972fo","text":"Surfaces in the lab should be cleaned three times. Once with warm water and starch-free detergent, then with 5% NaOH, followed by a final cleaning with distilled water.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bhvuu","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d8ai0","text":"Saliva donor criteria","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":21}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fjovm","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f98i0","text":"Must have no/limited history of dental caries","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bc39v","text":" Must not have used antibiotics in the past 6 months","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"156me","text":"Abstain from oral hygiene 24 hours prior to donation","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e65f8","text":"Refrain from eating and drinking (except water) 2 hours before donation","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f9gg2","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"66be6","text":"For experiments involving starches, donors should avoid eating starch-containing foods on the day of donation. To make this more bearable, saliva donation should take place in the morning before steps: Saliva donors rinse their mouth with water for 30 seconds.
Stimulate saliva production by chewing tasteless gum or parafilm.
Collect the saliva by spitting into 50 ml plastic centrifuge tubes. Make sure donors wear gloves to avoid contamination with non-oral bacteria.
Make a 2-fold dilution of saliva in sterile 20 % (v/v), glycerol and vortex the solution.
Before inoculation, vortex the saliva solution again.
Pipette the saliva solution into the wells, so approx. 1-2 cm of the substratum is submerged.
Place the plate in the incubator at 36ºC for 4 hours for static inoculation 04:00:00
After inoculation, transfer the samples to a new plate containing the artificial saliva, and place in a shaking incubator at 30 rpm, 36 °C, for 04:00:00 Artificial saliva by Bjørn Peare Bartholdy

Transfer the samples to a plate containing a 5% (m/v) Sucrose Contributed by users solution for 00:06:00 , then transfer back to the artificial saliva and leave Overnight . Plates with lids (substratum) in the incubator. Sucrose treatment plates covered with sterilised foil at the back.

First thing in the morning, transfer the samples to a new plate containing a 5% (m/v) Sucrose Contributed by users solution for 00:06:00 . While in the sucrose solution, add more artificial saliva to the wells on the original plate that have been partially depleted overnight.
After the 6 mins. return the samples to the plate with artificial saliva , and cover up the sucrose plate and leave for 08:00:00 .

After 8 hours, transfer the samples back to the plate with 5% (m/v) Sucrose Contributed by users solution for 00:06:00 . Transfer back to the artificial saliva and leave Overnight . Dispose of the sucrose.
Repeat steps from saliva collection and Day 0: Inoculation and feeding. A layer of clear plaque should be visible on the substrata
Prepare a new plate with artificial saliva. Transfer the samples from the inoculation plate to the artificial saliva .

Repeat steps 10 through 12 go to step #10 Prepare a new plate of artificial saliva every third day throughout the experiment. Every other morning, top up the wells with artificial saliva (so ca. 1-2 mm of the substratum is submerged).
Repeat steps 1 through 9 go to step #1
Repeat steps 10 through 12 go to step #10

Transfer the samples to a plate with the starch treatment(s) for 00:06:00 at 60 rpm, 36 °C, Example setup: Potato Potato Potato Potato Potato Potato Wheat Wheat Wheat Wheat Wheat Wheat Potato + Wheat Potato + Wheat Potato + Wheat Potato + Wheat Potato + Wheat Potato + Wheat Control Control Control Control Control Control If you are using multiple starch treatments within a single plate, take care to avoid cross-contamination.
Transfer the samples back to the artificial saliva plate for 08:00:00 at 30 rpm, 36 °C,
Transfer the samples to a plate with the starch treatment(s) for 00:06:00 at 60 rpm, 36 °C,
Transfer the samples back to the artificial saliva plate at 30 rpm, 36 °C, and leave Overnight
Transfer the samples to a plate containing the calcium phosphate monofluorophosphate urea (CPMU) solution for 00:06:00 at 60 rpm, 36 °C, CPMU by Bjørn Peare Bartholdy

Transfer the samples back to the artificial saliva for 02:00:00 at 30 rpm, 36 °C, Put a lid on the plate with CPMU (or cover with foil) to prevent evaporation. Repeat step 22 and 23, four more times every two hours.
Transfer the samples to a plate with the starch treatment(s) for 00:06:00 at 60 rpm, 36 °C,
Transfer the samples back to the artificial saliva plate at 30 rpm, 36 °C, and leave Overnight
Samples should be dried before sampling. Transfer to a new plate with no liquid and leave in the incubator.
Once dried, samples are processed like archaeological samples.
creator: Bjørn Peare Bartholdy
